授業目的: Please refer to the English section.
到達目標: Please refer to the English section.
授業の概要・背景: Spring Intensive English is a twice-a-week course designed to acclimate advanced proficiency first-year students to standard university English course expectations. Students will actively use English as a tool to complete various types of tasks. This will allow students to improve their English skills (reading, writing, listening, speaking), as well as other skills, such as organizing ideas, group work, public speaking etc.
授業方法: This class will be completed entirely in English. Through lectures, seminars, discussions, presentations and pair and group work, this course aims to improve students' overall English abilities in all facets of language competency, thus students can anticipate extensive group and pair work, revolving around discussion and critical thinking, and synthesizing and presenting ideas.Students will need a Gmail account to access Google Classroom. Most assignments and quizzes/short tests will be collected online. IMPORTANT: You must have space in your Google Drive. If your Google Drive is full (15GB) of photos, videos, or other documents, you will not be able to see class material or assignments. Please note that course schedule may change depending on project requirements.

トピック.授業計画1:
Course sessions may change depending on the needs of the class.
Students are required join actively and engage in communicative pair and group work. 
Students are expected to complete all assignments even if they are absent from thew face-to-face class.
トピック.授業外学習2: Students are expected to do 60-90 minutes of outside study outside per class session.

教科書/Required texts0:
No Textbook 
Please purchase a 6-month Xreading card from the Kwansei Gakuin Coop.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
Each subject is placed on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. 
By studying this subject with the DP and CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school. 
The DP, CP and curriculum table (list of subjects, etc.) of each faculty and graduate school are posted in the link below, so please use it as a reference for your study plan. 
https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1
